    <item>
      <title>Nuggets extend winning streak despite resting starters vs. Thunder</title>
      <description>The Denver Nuggets didn't care enough about playoff seeding to play any of their starters Friday night. After the Oklahoma City Thunder sat nine rotation players themselves, they came away with their 11th straight game and clinched home-court advantage in the first round.</description>
      <pubDate>11 Apr 2026 10:10:38 -0400</pubDate>
      <link>https://www.yardbarker.com/nba/articles/nuggets_extend_winning_streak_despite_resting_starters_vs_thunder/s1_13132_43713271</link>

        <media:credit> Ron Chenoy-Imagn Images </media:credit>
        <media:title> Oklahoma City Thunder forward Brooks Barnhizer (23) defends on Denver Nuggets guard Julian Strawther (3) in the second half at Ball Arena.  </media:title>
      </media:content>
      <category>NBA</category>
      <category>Denver Nuggets</category>
      <category>Oklahoma City Thunder</category>
      <category>Nikola Jokic</category>
    </item>

    <item>
      <title>Hawks choose first-round matchup with Knicks on season's final day</title>
      <description>With a win on the final day of the NBA season, the Atlanta Hawks could have clinched fifth place in the East and a first-round matchup with the Cleveland Cavaliers. Instead, they sat their starters and let fate — and the Boston Celtics — decide their playoff seed.</description>
